rationale: X is slightly stronger overall. Both abstracts define a testable gap, specify cohort design, comparator groups, primary outcome, event rates, p values, and adjusted OR with CI for acute success. X has better JACC fit through conventional section styling and a more restrained conclusion. Y is marginally clearer in methods wording, but its final sentence overextends the retrospective registry design by recommending incorporation of body size into procedural risk-benefit assessment, especially given very low MAE counts and an arbitrary 30 kg threshold.
loser_weakness: Y most lacked certainty calibration. The evidence supports association and hypothesis generation, while the conclusion moves toward practice assessment despite retrospective design, rare events, and limited detail on adjusted MAE modeling.
